summary: This chunk details the Munich Handbook’s (CLM 849) necromantic rituals, focusing on weekday-based conjurations of angels and spirits. It lists angelic rulers for each day (Raphael/Sol to Captiel/Saturnus), their attendant spirits, planetary correspondences, seals, suffumigations (e.g., assandalum croceum for Sunday, aloes for Monday), and hour-specific divine names and functions. The text includes detailed invocations using layered divine names (Adonay, Sadaye, Cados, Yma, etc.), instructions for image-making (e.g., silver dog for tongue-binding), and references to protective circles inscribed with Trinitarian formulas and angelic names.
key_passages: Coniuro super te, Samael, angelo qui est prepositus diei tercio, scilicet Marti, quod pro me laboretis et adimpleatis omnem meam voluntatem et totam meam peticionem.
Die Martis regnat et seruit rex filius dyabuli. Coadiutores sunt Carmath, Utanaual, Pascami.
Suffumigium dies dominice assandalum croceum vel rubeum uel simile sibi. Die Lune est aloes, anacap, uel simile sibi. Die Martis est piper aut abana...
Hec sunt nomina: Lyara, Lyalguana, Loaffar, Vialuarab, Lebara, Lebarion, Layasales. Hec sunt vij nomina que scribes in ventre forme.
In die dominico operari poteris ad ligandas li[n]gwas aut ad alias ligaciones hominum. Die Lune operandum est ad beniuolenciam et ad concordiam...
